questions.question: Define checked and unchecked exceptions in Java.
keyPoints: Checked exceptions: checked by compiler, e.g., IOException, SQLException
Unchecked exceptions: not checked by compiler, e.g., NullPointerException, ArithmeticException

questions.question: Explain the try-catch block in Java with an example.
keyPoints: try block: contains code that may throw an exception
catch block: handles the exception thrown by the try block

questions.question: Compare and contrast checked and unchecked exceptions in Java.
keyPoints: Definition of checked and unchecked exceptions
Examples of checked exceptions (e.g., IOException, SQLException)
Examples of unchecked exceptions (e.g., NullPointerException, ArithmeticException)
Difference in handling checked and unchecked exceptions

questions.question: Explain the use of multiple catch blocks and the finally block in Java with an example.
keyPoints: Multiple catch blocks: handle different types of exceptions
finally block: always executes, used for cleanup
Example code demonstrating multiple catch blocks and finally block

questions.question: Discuss the advantages of using exception handling in Java programming.
